# Architecture Checklist: Foundation for Future Integrations

**Purpose**: Validate whether the architecture, configuration, dependency-boundary, and operability requirements are written clearly enough to guide implementation of the Worker Service baseline.
**Created**: 2026-07-03
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the required startup responsibilities for `Program.cs` fully specified, including host creation, configuration loading, and worker registration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK002 Are the structural responsibilities for `Worker.cs` fully specified beyond "periodic execution", including safe looping and cancellation expectations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Are the expected responsibilities of `FaturamentoOrchestrator` explicitly documented as a boundary rather than an implementation placeholder? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK004 Are the reserved responsibilities of `Infrastructure/Storage`, `Infrastructure/Automation`, and `Infrastructure/Email` each documented clearly enough to avoid speculative implementations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification explicitly define what must exist in the `Domain/Models` and `Domain/Interfaces` areas for this baseline, or is that scope intentionally minimal? [Gap, Spec §FR-001]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "startup path" defined with enough specificity to distinguish host bootstrapping requirements from runtime worker behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK007 Is "explicit recurring execution interval configuration" described with enough precision to determine where validation happens and what qualifies as incomplete input? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK008 Is "clear startup or validation failure" measurable enough to distinguish configuration rejection from silent fallback behavior? [Measurability, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK009 Are "logs, metrics, and tracing signals" defined with enough specificity to identify required signal categories without forcing premature implementation detail? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK010 Is "no additional authentication or authorization behavior" bounded clearly enough to avoid accidental inclusion of secret-management abstractions or operational security scaffolding? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do the structural constraints in the spec align with the plan decision to keep `Infrastructure` directories free of per-area placeholder adapters? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK012 Do the edge-case expectations for missing interval configuration align with the measurable outcome requiring failure communication before normal execution begins? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Do the architecture tasks preserve the clarified restriction against adding Playwright or MailKit business logic in this increment? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Plan §Technical Context, Tasks §Phase 3-5]
- [x] CHK014 Are the plan's assumptions about a single root-level project consistent with the source-tree expectations described in the specification? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Project Structure]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the "identify the correct location within 5 minutes" outcome be objectively reviewed without relying on unstated reviewer expertise? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK016 Is "single uninterrupted path with no missing structural handoff points" defined clearly enough to evaluate architectural readiness objectively? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK017 Is the requirement that 100% of named integration categories have reserved locations traceable to explicit paths in the written structure? [Traceability, Spec §SC-003, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK018 Can the observability success criterion be verified from written requirements alone, or does it need a more explicit mapping of required startup, scheduling, and configuration-failure signals? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for the alternate scenario where the service starts correctly but future infrastructure adapters remain absent for multiple releases? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements defined for the exception scenario where configuration is present but semantically invalid, not just missing? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK021 Are recovery expectations defined after a startup configuration failure, such as whether operators are expected only to correct configuration and restart? [Gap, Recovery, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements explicit about what parts of observability are mandatory in this increment versus intentionally deferred to later phases? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK023 Are the package dependency requirements written clearly enough to distinguish mandatory compile-time references from out-of-scope runtime integration behavior? [Clarity, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Decision 4]
- [x] CHK024 Is the assumption of local-only structural preparation consistent with the expectation for metrics and tracing seams, or does that boundary need tighter wording? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK025 Are external prerequisites such as .NET 8 SDK and NuGet restore availability captured as assumptions or explicit implementation prerequisites where needed? [Dependency, Gap, Quickstart §Prerequisites]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Does the spec define whether empty marker files, README placeholders, or directory-only structures are all acceptable interpretations of "estrutura de pastas" for this increment? [Ambiguity, Spec §US1, Tasks §Phase 1]
- [x] CHK027 Is there any conflict between keeping infrastructure directories empty and still requiring explicit extension points for DI registration and observability seams? [Conflict, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK028 Is the boundary between implementation-ready architecture and implementation detail stated clearly enough to keep future tasks from inventing extra abstractions? [Ambiguity, Plan §Summary, Tasks §Implementation Strategy]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- This checklist is intentionally rigorous and author-oriented, focused on technical and architectural requirement quality rather than implementation behavior
